businessSummary.oneLiner: The world's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded far beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD has shifted to a $99/month subscription model, and key growth catalysts are lined up for April 2026: Cybercab production start, Roadster reveal, and Q1 earnings.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extremely elevated vs. S&P 500 average of ~24x and auto sector average of ~12x. Reflects AI/robotics/autonomy premium, but vulnerable to earnings misses

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS of $2.08. Still rich but half the TTM multiple, reflecting market expectations of an earnings rebound

keyMetrics.name: Revenue (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 2.93% YoY. 2026 consensus revenue of $105.7B implies 11.5% growth reacceleration

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed to legacy OEM levels by price competition. Energy expansion and FSD subscriptions are key to margin recovery

keyMetrics.name: Free Cash Flow (FCF)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation even after $8.53B in capex. Ample room for Cybercab and Terafab investments

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Net cash position of $29.3B. Best-in-class balance sheet

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $20-25B Joint Chip Factory Launch (3/21)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI officially launched a $20-25B joint semiconductor fab in Austin on March 21. Targeting 1 terawatt annual compute output by 2027. However, analysts note only TSMC, Samsung, and Intel can produce chips at this scale, and no construction timeline was provided

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Starting April
growthDrivers.description: Steerless fully autonomous robotaxi. NHTSA approval in progress. A new revenue stream upon mass production

growthDrivers.title: Roadster Reveal in April
growthDrivers.description: Musk announced on March 17 it will be 'the most insane thing ever.' Strong brand halo effect expected

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Deal
growthDrivers.description: Next-gen AI chip production deal with Samsung. Reduces NVIDIA dependency through in-house AI infrastructure

growthDrivers.title: FSD Subscription Model ($99/mo)
growthDrivers.description: Shifted from $12K one-time purchase to $99/month subscription. 8.4B miles of driving data powering recurring revenue

growthDrivers.title: China Sales Surge (+35% YoY)
growthDrivers.description: China sales up 35% YoY in Jan-Feb 2026. Sustained growth momentum in the world's largest EV market

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Samsung AI6 foundry deal for AI chip internalization

moats.description: 8.4B miles of real-world FSD driving data. An insurmountable lead that competitors cannot replicate in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Competitors including Ford and GM use Tesla's charging infrastructure
From battery cells and AI chips (Terafab) to software and charging, Tesla's full vertical integration delivers cost advantages and rapid innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader, catching up to Tesla in global EV sales. Superior price competitiveness

competitors.name: Rivian
competitors.detail: Competing in US electric pickup/SUV segment. Has exclusive Amazon delivery vehicle contract

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in autonomous driving commercialization. Expanding robotaxi services

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV market share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Terafab feasibility questioned: analysts say only 3 firms can make chips at this scale
recentNews.significance: Electrek called Terafab 'Battery Day on steroids' and experts noted only TSMC, Samsung, and Intel can produce at this scale. No construction timeline provided, raising execution concerns

recentNews.headline: Terafab $20-25B joint fab officially launched in Austin
recentNews.significance: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI launched the joint semiconductor factory. Musk called it 'the most ambitious chip project in history,' targeting 1 terawatt annual compute by 2027

recentNews.headline: Netherlands FSD testing complete, approval delayed to April 10
recentNews.significance: First European FSD test completed in the Netherlands, but formal approval pushed from 3/20 to 4/10. A gateway to European autonomous driving market

recentNews.headline: Jury finds Musk defrauded Twitter investors during acquisition
recentNews.significance: Federal jury ruled Musk artificially depressed Twitter's stock price. Appeal planned but heightens CEO risk

recentNews.headline: NHTSA expands FSD investigation for reduced visibility conditions
recentNews.significance: Federal probe expanded over autonomous driving safety concerns. Potential risk to Cybercab approval

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush's Daniel Ives maintains $600 target, calling Tesla the top Physical AI beneficiary. Motley Fool notes 10 of 34 analysts have sell ratings, an unusually large number

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: TTM P/E of 364x is 15x the S&P 500 average (24x) and 30x the auto sector (12x). Any earnings disappointment could trigger sharp declines

risks.title: Musk Legal Risk Escalation
risks.description: Jury found Musk liable for defrauding Twitter investors (3/20). Appeal planned but compounds CEO risk alongside DOGE activities and boycott movement

risks.title: Terafab Execution Uncertainty
risks.description: Experts question the $20-25B fab's feasibility. No construction timeline provided. Only 3 companies globally can produce chips at this scale. Risk of another unfulfilled promise like Battery Day

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Investigation Expansion
risks.description: Federal probe expanded on FSD safety in reduced visibility. Could delay Cybercab approval or trigger recalls

risks.title: Price Competition Intensifying
risks.description: Margin pressure from Chinese competitors like BYD continues. Gross margin compressed to 18%

risks.title: Revenue Decline
risks.description: TTM revenue down 2.93% YoY. 2026 growth rebound expected but uncertain

buyReasons.title: Physical AI Platform Transformation
buyReasons.rationale: Evolving from an EV company into a platform spanning autonomy, robotics, and AI chips. Success in any segment could trigger valuation re-rating

buyReasons.title: Concentrated 2026 Growth Catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: April brings Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Terafab activation, and Q1 earnings (4/21). Multiple catalysts compressed into a short window

buyReasons.title: FSD Subscription Recurring Revenue
buyReasons.rationale: 8.4B miles of data powering FSD at $99/month subscription. Continuous revenue stream beyond vehicle sales

buyReasons.title: Financial Strength
buyReasons.rationale: Net cash of $29.3B and FCF of $6.2B provide ample capacity for major investments

buyReasons.title: Technical Pullback from March Highs
buyReasons.rationale: Down 17% from March highs above $440. Trading below both 50-day and 200-day moving averages, setting up for potential technical bounce

overallOpinion: Terafab $20-25B chip factory launched, but experts question execution: no timeline, only 3 firms globally can produce at this scale, and it risks becoming another Battery Day
April is packed with catalysts: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Q1 earnings (4/21), and Netherlands FSD approval (4/10). The direction will be clearer after these events
Musk's Twitter fraud jury verdict (3/20), NHTSA FSD probe expansion, and P/E of 364x create significant downside pressure
At $367.96, down 17% from March highs. Below both 50-day ($398) and 200-day ($406) moving averages. Wait for April catalyst confirmation before entering

investmentVerdict.summary: Terafab launch and April growth catalysts (Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings) boost upside potential, but execution doubts, Musk's legal risks, and the extreme P/E of 364x weigh heavily. With the stock down 17% from March highs, the risk-reward favors waiting for April event confirmation.

x.hook: Terafab launched but experts are skeptical
x.text:
$TSLA (Mar 23) Launched a $25B chip factory. But analysts say 'only 3 firms can make chips at this scale.' No timeline given. $367.96, down 17% from March highs.

1/4
thread:
April lineup: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Q1 earnings (4/21), Netherlands FSD approval (4/10). Catalysts are there, but Musk's Twitter fraud verdict is a headwind.

2/4

P/E at 364x, revenue declining (-2.93%), NHTSA expanding FSD probe. As many headwinds as tailwinds right now.

3/4

Below 50-day ($398) and 200-day ($406) averages. Consensus target $389 (+5.7%). Wait for April catalysts before making a move.

4/4

witty.text:
$TSLA (Mar 23) Opened a $25B chip factory. Stock's at $367.96, down 17% from March highs. Experts say only 3 companies on Earth can pull this off. Musk says 'hold my beer.'

1/4
thread:
April has 4 lottery tickets: Cybercab, Roadster, earnings, FSD approval. Musk is great at promises. The question is always delivery.

2/4

P/E 364x with declining revenue. Twitter fraud verdict on top. Tesla shareholders have stress-tested resilience at this point.

3/4

Below both moving averages. Target $389 (+5.7%). Battery Day deja vu or actual turning point? April will tell.

4/4

empathy.hook: Tesla holders, good news but stock keeps falling
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 23) Launched a $25B factory and the stock still dropped. $367.96, down 17% from highs. If you're holding, the frustration is understandable.

1/4
thread:
Fraud verdict, FSD probe, skeptics everywhere. When bad news piles up, it's natural to feel uncertain.

2/4

But April brings real catalysts: Cybercab, Roadster, earnings. This time might be different. Or not. That's the honest truth.

3/4

Below 50-day and 200-day averages. Target $389 (+5.7%). April will set the direction. Hang in there.

4/4
